name: Morphik
tagline: Multimodal RAG for visually rich documents — search text, diagrams, tables, and scanned PDFs
seoTitle: Morphik - Multimodal RAG for Visual Documents
metaDescription: Morphik is a source-available multimodal RAG stack that ingests and searches visually rich documents — diagrams, tables, and scanned PDFs — via a Python SDK.
overview: Morphik is a RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) toolset built for visually rich and multimodal documents. Instead of flattening a file into plain text, it indexes the visual content of pages, so charts, diagrams, tables, and scanned PDFs stay searchable rather than getting mangled into unreadable strings.

It is aimed at developers who need to feed complex documents into an LLM application and want one system for ingestion, search, and querying instead of stitching together separate OCR, embedding, and vector-database tools. You can run it self-hosted with Docker or direct install, or use the managed service with a free tier.

Within the RAG-framework category, Morphik focuses on the multimodal side of retrieval. It uses techniques such as ColPali to understand page visuals, and exposes its functionality through a Python SDK, a REST API, a web console, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
features: Multimodal search over images, PDFs, and videos through a single endpoint, using ColPali-style retrieval that understands page visuals
Ingestion for unstructured and visually rich documents, including diagrams, tables, and scanned PDFs
Metadata extraction with bounding boxes, labeling, and classification
Integrations with existing tools and workflows such as Google Suite, Slack, and Confluence
Multiple access paths: Python SDK, REST API, a web console, and Model Context Protocol (MCP)
Self-hostable via Docker or direct install, plus a managed service with a free tier

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest path is the managed service: sign up for a free account at morphik.ai to get a Morphik URI, then use the Python SDK to ingest and query files. You can also self-host the open-source core via Docker or direct install.

steps.heading: Install the Python SDK
steps.body: Install the morphik package from PyPI.

steps.heading: Ingest a file
steps.body: Create a client with your Morphik URI and ingest a document. Morphik handles complex, visually rich files like PDFs.
steps.code:
from morphik import Morphik

morphik = Morphik("<your-morphik-uri>")
morphik.ingest_file("path/to/your/super/complex/file.pdf")

steps.heading: Query your data
steps.body: Ask questions in natural language and Morphik searches across the ingested content.
steps.code: morphik.query("What's the height of screw 14-A in the chair assembly instructions?")

useCases: Building a Q&A or chat application over technical manuals where answers depend on diagrams, tables, and figures rather than plain text
Searching across large collections of scanned PDFs and mixed text-and-image documents where traditional OCR pipelines lose accuracy
Extracting structured metadata — bounding boxes, labels, classifications — from unstructured documents at scale
Connecting an LLM to documents in tools like Google Suite, Slack, or Confluence through Morphik's integrations and MCP support
